message:
fix(session): harden linearity detection and corruption recovery in session store

Two improvements to session persistence reliability:

1. session.rs (finalize_loaded_entries): The is_linear flag previously
   only checked for branching (multiple children of a parent). This
   missed cases where multiple root entries exist or orphan entries
   are present — both of which indicate a non-linear session history
   that requires branch-aware traversal. Now is_linear requires:
   no branching AND at most one root AND no orphans. This prevents
   the fast-path linear replay from silently dropping entries in
   sessions that have been corrupted or have concurrent writers.

2. session_store_v2.rs (rebuild_index_inner): The corruption recovery
   logic previously had an asymmetry: oversized lines, missing-newline
   frames, parse errors, and non-monotonic sequences were only
   auto-recoverable if they occurred in the last segment file.
   Corruption in intermediate segments would return a hard error,
   leaving the session store in an unrecoverable state. Now all
   corruption types consistently truncate the affected segment and
   drop all subsequent segments, since data after corruption cannot
   be trusted regardless of which segment it occurs in. This makes
   crash recovery strictly more robust without changing the
   happy-path behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
